chief_complaint: A 60-year-old patient presented with angina-like chest pain.
history: The patient is an active smoker and a former type 2 diabetic with a history of chest pain and a positive stress test.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are mentioned.
labs: Initial electrocardiogram showed a discreet elevation of the ST segment and an aVR necrosis Q wave with mirror signs in the inferior territory. Diagnostic coronary angiography objectified a multi-vessel coronary lesion.
